metadata.name: Fireworks #181
metadata.description:
Every token can output in 12 different aspect ratios:
1x1, 3x1, 3x2, 2x3, 4x3, 3x4, 5x4, 4x5, 16x9, 9x16, 16x10, 10x16

With 3 framing options:
1. No frame
2. Rectangle frame
3. Arched window

And downloadable in 4 different resolutions:
1. 1000px
2. 2000px
3. 4000px
4. 8000px

Click on the image in live view to unlock all these possibilities.

Made with p5.js.
Primary split 5% with The Processing Foundation.

metadata.name: Glycerin #19
metadata.description:
Flow of pixel noise gets interrupted and displaced with random shapes.

----------
64 different color palettes.
Press 'S' to capture a 3840x3840 screenshot of the current frame.

XALTED 2022

metadata.name: Monopoles on ACID #7
metadata.description:
Monopoles simulation.

Several positive and negative monopoles are scattered around the canvas then the particles are allowed to travel along the field lines.

In this piece I implemented a monopole simulation. 

The project is written in vanilla Javascript.

The drawing is executed in a square at window resolution.

Press '2' to double the resolution. You can press again '2' to double once more;
Press '1' to get back at the window size.
Press 's' to save a png at the current resolution. You can also right click and 'save-as' to obtain the same result.


It uses two external packages, both licensed under MIT license:
* https://www.npmjs.com/package/file-saver
* https://www.npmjs.com/package/alea

metadata.name: Isogrid #232
metadata.description:
An exploration with rectangles in a isometric grid, playing with colors, alpha's, gradients and blendmodes.

Press e to save a 4000x4000 PNG in live mode.

Lammetje May 2022

metadata.name: HappyFx #239
metadata.description:
This is my tribute to 1 year FxHash aniversity 🎉
The project has 30 color palettes that where used last year in various projects.

50% of primary sales wil be donated to the Processing Foundation.

Press s in live mode to download.

Download a higher resolution by adding to the URL:
&density=2 / &density=3 / &density=4

Lammetje november 2022, NFT License

metadata.name: Maru #103
metadata.description:
Maru means “circle” in Japanese, it also represents many other things like integrity, infinity and the universe. The art generated through my exploration with circles felt like it needed this name.

Best viewed in full screen, Press s in live mode for a 1414x2000 download

Download a higher resolution by adding to the URL:
&density=2 / &density=3 / &density=4

Lammetje Oktober 2022

metadata.name: Days #94
metadata.description:
Days by Asaf Slook
Created using p5.js and love :)

metadata.name: Hypericum #236
metadata.description:
This system translates color into movement. A swarm of drawing particles, like virtual pencils, are guided by the color values of the pixels in an input image. The formulas performing the conversion put into play many parameters. The space of possibilities of the system is enormous, the variety of drawings endless. 256 is just a small arbitrary sample. 

This work can be seen as a playful reflection on the concept of “style”, which is so essential to our ideas about art and (human) artists. This short program can paint the same image on infinitely many “styles” – each of them reducible to a simple combination of mathematical parameters. 

The input image is a photo of a yellow hypericum flower I took during a holiday in the Argentinean Patagonia, near Bariloche, in 2016.

Better on landscape format (optimal ratio is 3:2) > on your computer, [open live]
Press [s] to download capture.

Made by Leonardo Solaas
TW > @solaas
IG > @leonardo_solaas
WWW > solaas.com.ar

metadata.name: fxhash x thi.ng project template #875
metadata.description:
THIS IS NOT AN ARTWORK (at least not intentional)!

This NFT acts as a donation/funding mechanism for a new project template Git repo, providing a compact, ready-to-go scaffolding for generative art projects aimed at the fx(hash) platform/marketplace.

https://github.com/thi-ng/fxhash-tpl

This template evolved over time and has served as basis for some of the author's own NFT projects (https://www.fxhash.xyz/u/toxi). The template is aimed at TypeScript (but can be easily adapted to plain JavaScript) and, at the time of writing, uses Vite as dev & build tool, ensuring an altogether great & speedy workflow with minimal fuzz.

(In the future, I'm aiming to provide an alternative version for more advanced hybrid projects using TypeScript, Zig (https://ziglang.org) and WebAssembly (https://webassembly.org/)...)

Framework agnostic

Even though several packages from the https://thi.ng/umbrella ecosystem are used to provide various essential overarching functionality, the template is organized in such a way that the main parts are completely framework-agnostic. You should be able to easily plug in your own toolkit of choice, however I cannot promise to provide support for this myself (though happy to accept PRs, also in terms of further docs, how-tos, guides etc.)

Example project

A tiny, fully documented throwaway example sketch is included, illustrating the following:

- Overall project structure & build commands
- FXhash related setup
- Static & dynamic configuration and state (re)initialization (via dependency graph)
- FXhash PRNG wrapper & utilities
- Canvas & SVG conversion/export/download
- 2D geometry creation, transformation & drawing
- Basic vector algebra example usage


Keyboard shortcuts

- space :  pause/play animation
- x : download canvas as PNG
- s : download SVG version

In case of doubt, it's this example project what is used here for minting. The funding, however, will be used for the further maintenance of both this project template and supporting my other open source work. 20% are also donated to the FXhash development team itself.

Thank you for your support! 🙏😍

metadata.name: Neuro-Daguerreotypes #91
metadata.description:
Landscapes with islands lost in the fog are old neuro-daguerreotypes.
Their surface changes, developing from rocks, fern forests, to cities and metropolises that gradually fall into decay, ruins, turn into slums or shelters of other creatures.
The landscape of the islands includes:
- rocks
- hills
- dwellings of elves
- ruins
- villages by the sea
- ghettos and slums
- metropolises and skyscrapers
When viewed from space, by and large, nothing changes, but in rare cases you will be able to see the whole picture.

Artwork is based on PNG layers (with masks), Disco Diffusion, python and p5.js code.

metadata.name: Kintsugi #231
metadata.description:
Kintsugi is a philosophy, it's the concept of highlighting imperfections, visualizing patches and seams on the area to focus on, rather than focusing on missing details.

This art of precious scars is a technique used as a means of analyzing the idea of ​​loss through synthesis and restoration of the destroyed and a metaphor for the possibility of rebirth.
Recovery from destructive events is possible through care and patching, not through hiding the damage, but by accepting a new state of the object or the whole life.
The world is a vase that is endlessly disassembled and sewn together again, continuing to bear traces of each event.

Non-human entities helped us put these vases together. In the beginning, there were vague forms, these were the shards themselves, created in collaboration with Latent Diffusion. Over time, they have grown in detail, drawing their stories more clearly, thanks to Jax Diffusion.

The code that puts everything together was written by Yulikolay, for which he is greatly appreciated.

metadata.name: ✂️ Ink Cuts ✂️ #68
metadata.description:
A series of beautiful polar functions plotted in ink and then cut to pieces. 

FEATURES

- 11 Different Polar Function Shapes
- Blue and Black Ink Variations
- 4 Background Variations
- Numerous possible paper cuts


NOTE

This piece takes a moment to render, please be patient.

metadata.name: RGB Elementary Cellular Automaton #431
metadata.description:
Stephen Wolfram's Elementary Cellular Automaton where 3 different rules are drawn in the Red, Green and Blue color components of the image.

Rules are selected randomly among a pre-selection of 53 rules from which nice behaviors emerge.

Tokens can have a cell size in the [1; 20] range.

objkt.name: Ask Me About The Dimensional Shift #167

metadata.name: Ask Me About The Dimensional Shift #167
metadata.description:
Inspired by the Many-Worlds Interpretation of quantum mechanics. 
Your minted artwork will transform based on your decisions on how to view it.
There are no rarity attributes assigned to this artwork, its value is intended to be determined by viewer aesthetic alone.

This artwork was created using p5js.

linktr.ee/lisaorthstudio
